steps.title: Extract the line of best fit from the graph for data set E
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at $x = 0$, where $y = 12$, so the y-intercept is 12. Reading another point: at $x = 4$, the line passes through approximately $(4, 18)$. The slope is $\frac{18 - 12}{4 - 0} = \frac{6}{4} = 1.5$. The equation for data set E is $y = 12 + 1.5x$.

steps.title: Apply the scaling transformation to both components
steps.content:
When all y-coordinates are multiplied by 3.9, the equation transforms as follows:
  • Y-intercept: $12 \times 3.9 = 46.8$
  • Slope: $1.5 \times 3.9 = 5.85 \approx 5.9$
The line of best fit for data set F is $y = 46.8 + 5.9x$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
